basics.name: Carlos Mendes
basics.label: Executive Chef
basics.summary: Executive chef specializing in modern Portuguese cuisine with Mediterranean influences. Fifteen years leading brigades in Michelin-starred kitchens and boutique hotel restaurants.

work.name: Quinta do Atlântico
work.description: Luxury boutique hotel with 40-seat tasting-menu restaurant
work.summary: Lead kitchen brigade of 14; responsible for menu, suppliers, food cost, and HACCP compliance.
highlights: Earned first Michelin star in 2023; retained in 2024 and 2025.
Reduced food waste 35% through root-to-leaf programme and compost partnership.
Curated wine pairing programme with 120-label cellar focusing on Douro and Vinho Verde.

work.name: Restaurante Maré Alta
work.description: Seafood-focused fine dining on the Tagus waterfront
work.summary: Second-in-command; ran hot line during service and managed fish butchery.
highlights: Developed signature octopus confit dish featured in national food press.
Trained 8 commis chefs through Escola de Hotelaria partnership.

work.name: Hotel InterContinental Porto
work.description: Four-star hotel banquet and à la carte operations
work.summary: Managed cold kitchen, charcuterie, and banquet plating for events up to 300 guests.
highlights: Standardized mise en place reducing banquet setup time 20%.
Introduced local cheese board programme sourcing from Minho producers.

volunteer.summary: Prepare weekly meals from surplus ingredients for community dining programme.
highlights: Mentored culinary students on zero-waste cooking techniques.

education.institution: Escola de Hotelaria e Turismo do Porto

awards.title: Michelin Star – Quinta do Atlântico
awards.summary: One star: High quality cooking, worth a stop.

awards.title: Chef of the Year – Norte Region
awards.summary: Voted best emerging chef in Northern Portugal by panel of critics and sommeliers.

publications.name: Atlantic Ingredients: A Chef's Guide to Minho Produce
publications.summary: 200-page cookbook and supplier guide celebrating coastal and mountain ingredients of northern Portugal.

projects.name: Chef's Table Documentary Series
projects.description: Six-episode web series following suppliers from boat to plate along the Minho coast.
highlights: 500k views on YouTube in first season.

projects.name: Pop-up Collaboration – Lisbon Wine Week
projects.description: Five-course collaborative dinner with three Portuguese winemakers.

references.name: Ana Ribeiro, General Manager
references.reference: Carlos transformed our restaurant into a destination. His leadership, creativity, and respect for local producers define everything we serve.
